title: vulpia myuros subsp. bromoides
intro: Vulpia myuros subsp. bromoides, also known as Squirrel Tail or False Brome, is a type of grass-like plant that belongs to the family Poaceae. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Vulpia myuros subsp. bromoides include its ability to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its use in treating skin conditions like eczema and acne. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to promote overall health and well-being.
